title: Pip and Stone and the Loud Thing

pageTimings.text:
Pip and Stone were sitting outside the Hollow Oak.
Then a noise came from the Fog Marsh.
It was very, very loud.

"I want to find out what it is," said Pip.
"I do not," said Stone.
"It is very loud."

Pip walked toward the Fog Marsh.
He looked back.
Stone had not moved.
Stone had moved one foot.

At the edge of the marsh, the noise came again.
It was even louder.
Pip's hair stood on end.
Stone appeared behind him.
"I came anyway," said Stone.

They stood at the edge together.
The fog was thick and grey-green.
The noise bounced off the twisted trees.
"Together," said Stone.
Pip nodded.

Inside the marsh, the noise was everywhere.
The reeds shook.
The water rippled.
They crept closer.

On a mossy stone in the marsh sat a toadling.
It was very small.
Its mouth was very wide open.
All the noise was coming from it.

The toadling stopped when it saw them.
Everything was suddenly very quiet.
"Oh," said Pip.
"Oh," said Stone.

"Why were you shouting?" asked Pip.
The toadling blinked.
"I am lost," it said.
"I was calling for my family."
Its voice was very small now.

Stone knelt down carefully.
"We will help," said Stone.
The toadling looked at Stone.
"You are very large," said the toadling.
"Yes," said Stone. "But I am gentle."

Stone stood up and called out across the marsh.
It was the loudest sound Pip had ever heard.
Louder than the toadling.
Much louder.
Something answered from the far side of the marsh.

Back at the Hollow Oak, everyone asked:
"What was the loud thing?"
"Very small," said Pip.
"Very loud," said Stone.
They both smiled.
